title: salvia mellifera subsp. revoluta
intro: Salvia mellifera subsp. revoluta, also known as the black sage or California sagebrush, is a subspecies of the Salvia mellifera plant, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its compact growth habit and small, blackish-purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Salvia mellifera subsp. revoluta include its potential to reduce anxiety and stress, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and salves, and its essential oil is used in aromatherapy and perfumery, although its use should be limited to small amounts and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
